Asclepias feayi, a plant species within the Apocynaceae family, is a type of milkweed native to the Americas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be beneficial in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Asclepias feayi include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, this plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.

The genus Asclepiodora is a part of the Apocynaceae family and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Asclepiodora feayi is a type of milkweed vine that produces clusters of small, fragrant flowers. It is a perennial plant with a twining habit, often found in humid and shaded environments. This classification places Asclepiodora feayi within the broader Apocynaceae family and the Asclepiodora genus.
